reason: The provided frames show product equipment/supplies display (Lincoln Logs, borescope, alligator forceps, copper tube) in the first two images, followed by office/desk environment shots with a computer monitor displaying what appears to be laparoscopic video content. However, the actual laparoscopic task video is being displayed ON a monitor in an office setting rather than being the primary recording. The frames show a secondary/indirect recording of a task video through a computer screen, which creates poor visibility, glare, and angle distortion. Additionally, the content visible on the monitor (wooden blocks/connector pieces with clamps and instruments) does not clearly correspond to any standard FLS task (Peg Transfer, Pattern Cut, Endoloop, or Extracorporeal/Intracorporeal Suture tasks). The task depicted appears to be a non-standard training or demonstration setup, possibly a DIY/low-cost trainer setup rather than an official FLS box trainer.
confidence_rationale: Video quality is severely compromised by indirect recording through a monitor screen. Equipment and setup do not match standard FLS trainer box specifications. Task identity cannot be reliably determined from the obscured and distorted monitor display.

frame_analyses.description: Monitor display shows two brass/metal cylinders and white instrument

cannot_score_reasons: Task is displayed on a computer monitor rather than being directly recorded
Secondary screen recording introduces glare, distortion, and poor visibility
Equipment setup does not match official FLS task trainer box specifications
Task type cannot be reliably identified from obscured monitor display
Frame-by-frame analysis of actual task performance is not possible due to indirect recording method
recommendation: Please provide a direct recording of the FLS task performance from the trainer box camera/phone camera, not a recording of a monitor displaying the task. This will allow proper scoring according to official FLS rubrics.
